Docs
Core Concepts

Core Concepts

# Overview

JephyPHP Framework Documentation follows a clean, modular architecture that separates concerns while maintaining high performance and flexibility. This section explains the core architectural patterns and how different components interact to handle HTTP requests

Routing

Routing maps HTTP requests to specific controller methods. Jephy-MVC provides a flexible and expressive router that supports RESTful routes, parameters, middleware, and route groups.

Basic Routing

Define routes in jephy-mvc/routes.php:


php

// Basic GET route
$router->get('/', 'HomeController@index');

// Basic POST route
$router->post('/contact', 'ContactController@submit');

// Basic PUT route
$router->put('/users/{id}', 'UserController@update');

// Basic DELETE route
$router->delete('/users/{id}', 'UserController@delete');

Route Parameters

php

use App\Core\Router;

// Required parameters
$router->get('/users/{id}', 'UserController@show');
// Example: /users/123 -> $id = 123

// Multiple parameters
$router->get('/posts/{category}/{slug}', 'PostController@show');
// Example: /posts/tech/my-article -> $category = 'tech', $slug = 'my-article'

// Optional parameters (using regex)
$router->get('/profile/{id?}', 'ProfileController@show');
// Example: /profile/123 -> $id = 123, /profile -> $id = null

Named Routes

 php
 
 // Define named route
$router->get('/dashboard', 'DashboardController@index')->name('dashboard');

// Generate URL from route name
$url = $router->route('dashboard'); // Returns '/dashboard'
$url = $router->route('user.profile', ['id' => 123]); // '/users/123/profile'

Route Groups

// Group with prefix
$router->group(['prefix' => 'admin'], function($router) {
    $router->get('/users', 'AdminController@users');
    $router->get('/settings', 'AdminController@settings');
    // Full URLs: /admin/users, /admin/settings
});

// Group with middleware
$router->group(['middleware' => ['AuthMiddleware', 'AdminMiddleware']], function($router) {
    $router->get('/dashboard', 'DashboardController@index');
    $router->get('/reports', 'ReportController@index');
});

// Nested groups
$router->group(['prefix' => 'api', 'middleware' => ['ApiMiddleware']], function($router) {
    $router->group(['prefix' => 'v1'], function($router) {
        $router->get('/users', 'Api\UserController@index');
        $router->get('/users/{id}', 'Api\UserController@show');
    });
});

HTTP Method Spoofing


// HTML forms only support GET and POST. Use _method parameter for others:
// In your form:
// 

// Route will still work
$router->put('/users/{id}', 'UserController@update');

Resource Controllers


// Define all resource routes at once
$router->resource('products', 'ProductController');
// Generates:
// GET    /products           -> index
// GET    /products/create    -> create
// POST   /products           -> store
// GET    /products/{id}      -> show
// GET    /products/{id}/edit -> edit
// PUT    /products/{id}      -> update
// DELETE /products/{id}      -> destroy

Route Caching


// In production, cache routes for performance
// jephy-mvc/core/Router.php
public function cache() {
    $routes = serialize($this->routes);
    file_put_contents(FRAMEWORK_PATH . '/storage/cache/routes.php', '<?php return ' . var_export($routes, true) . ';');
}

ORM - Active Record Styled

Jephy-MVC includes a lightweight Active Record implementation that maps database tables to PHP objects, making database operations intuitive and expressive.

What is Active Record?

Active Record is a design pattern where each database table corresponds to a PHP class, and each record instance represents a row in that table.

Basic Model Setup


php

// jephy-mvc/app/models/User.php
namespace App\Models;

use Core\Model;

class User extends Model
{
    // Table name (optional - defaults to pluralized class name)
    protected $table = 'users';
    
    // Primary key (defaults to 'id')
    protected $primaryKey = 'id';
    
    // Fillable fields (mass assignment protection)
    protected $fillable = ['name', 'email', 'password', 'role'];
    
    // Hidden fields (not shown in JSON)
    protected $hidden = ['password'];
    
    // Guarded fields (opposite of fillable)
    protected $guarded = ['id', 'created_at', 'updated_at'];
    
    // Timestamps (true by default)
    public $timestamps = true;
}

CRUD Operations


php

// CREATE - Create new record
$user = new User();
$user->name = 'John Doe';
$user->email = 'john@example.com';
$user->password = password_hash('secret', PASSWORD_DEFAULT);
$user->save();

// CREATE using mass assignment
$user = User::create([
    'name' => 'Jane Doe',
    'email' => 'jane@example.com',
    'password' => password_hash('secret', PASSWORD_DEFAULT)
]);

// READ - Find by ID
$user = User::find(1);

// READ - Find by attribute
$user = User::where('email', 'john@example.com')->first();
$users = User::where('role', 'admin')->get();

// READ - All records
$users = User::all();

// UPDATE
$user = User::find(1);
$user->name = 'John Updated';
$user->save();

// UPDATE using mass assignment
User::where('role', 'user')->update(['status' => 'inactive']);

// DELETE
$user = User::find(1);
$user->delete();

// DELETE by ID
User::destroy(1);
User::destroy([1, 2, 3]);

// DELETE with condition
User::where('status', 'inactive')->delete();

Query Builder Methods

php

// Select specific columns
$users = User::select('id', 'name', 'email')->get();

// Where conditions
$users = User::where('status', 'active')
            ->where('role', 'admin')
            ->get();

// Or where conditions
$users = User::where('status', 'active')
            ->orWhere('role', 'superadmin')
            ->get();

// Where between
$users = User::whereBetween('created_at', ['2024-01-01', '2024-12-31'])->get();

// Where in array
$users = User::whereIn('role', ['admin', 'editor'])->get();

// Where null
$users = User::whereNull('deleted_at')->get();

// Order by
$users = User::orderBy('name', 'asc')->orderBy('created_at', 'desc')->get();

// Limit and offset
$users = User::limit(10)->offset(20)->get();

// Pagination
$users = User::paginate(15, 1); // 15 per page, page 1

// Aggregates
$count = User::count();
$max = User::max('age');
$min = User::min('age');
$avg = User::avg('age');
$sum = User::sum('salary');

Relationships

// User model
class User extends Model
{
    // One-to-One
    public function profile()
    {
        return $this->hasOne(Profile::class, 'user_id');
    }
    
    // One-to-Many
    public function posts()
    {
        return $this->hasMany(Post::class, 'user_id');
    }
    
    // Many-to-Many
    public function roles()
    {
        return $this->belongsToMany(Role::class, 'user_roles', 'user_id', 'role_id');
    }
    
    // Belongs To (inverse of hasMany)
    public function department()
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(Department::class, 'dept_id');
    }
}

// Using relationships
$user = User::find(1);
$profile = $user->profile; // Access related profile
$posts = $user->posts; // Access all user posts

// Eager loading (prevent N+1 queries)
$users = User::with('profile', 'posts')->get();
foreach ($users as $user) {
    echo $user->profile->bio;
    foreach ($user->posts as $post) {
        echo $post->title;
    }
}

// Nested eager loading
$users = User::with('profile.settings', 'posts.comments')->get();

// Lazy eager loading
$users = User::all();
$users->load('profile', 'posts');

Soft Deletes

// Add SoftDelete trait to model
use Core\Traits\SoftDeletes;

class User extends Model
{
    use SoftDeletes;
    
    protected $dates = ['deleted_at'];
}

// Delete softly
$user->delete(); // Sets deleted_at timestamp

// Get including soft deleted
$users = User::withTrashed()->get();

// Get only soft deleted
$users = User::onlyTrashed()->get();

// Restore soft deleted
$user->restore();

// Force delete (permanent)
$user->forceDelete();

Scopes for Reusable Queries


// In model
class User extends Model
{
    // Local scope
    public function scopeActive($query)
    {
        return $query->where('status', 'active');
    }
    
    public function scopeAdmin($query)
    {
        return $query->where('role', 'admin');
    }
    
    public function scopeRecent($query, $days = 30)
    {
        return $query->where('created_at', '>=', now()->subDays($days));
    }
}

// Using scopes
$activeAdmins = User::active()->admin()->get();
$recentUsers = User::recent(7)->get();
$activeRecent = User::active()->recent()->get();

Raw Queries

php

// When you need raw SQL
$users = User::raw('SELECT * FROM users WHERE status = ?', ['active']);

// Using query builder with raw
$users = User::selectRaw('COUNT(*) as total, MONTH(created_at) as month')
            ->groupBy('month')
            ->get();

Dependency Injection

Dependency Injection (DI) is a design pattern where objects receive their dependencies from external sources rather than creating them internally. This promotes loose coupling, testability, and maintainability.

What is Dependency Injection?

Without Dependency Injection (Tight Coupling)


php

class UserController
{
    private $userService;
    
    public function __construct()
    {
        // Tight coupling - UserController is responsible for creating its dependencies
        $this->userService = new UserService();
    }
}

With Dependency Injection (Loose Coupling)


class UserController
{
    private $userService;
    
    // Dependencies are injected from outside
    public function __construct(UserService $userService)
    {
        $this->userService = $userService;
    }
}

Constructor Injection


php

namespace App\Controllers;

use App\Services\UserService;
use App\Repositories\UserRepository;
use App\Services\MailService;

class UserController extends Controller
{
    private $userService;
    private $userRepository;
    private $mailService;
    
    // All dependencies injected via constructor
    public function __construct(
        UserService $userService,
        UserRepository $userRepository,
        MailService $mailService
    ) {
        $this->userService = $userService;
        $this->userRepository = $userRepository;
        $this->mailService = $mailService;
    }
    
    public function show($id)
    {
        $user = $this->userService->getUserById($id);
        return $this->json($user);
    }
}

Setter Injection


class UserController extends Controller
{
    private $logger;
    
    // Optional dependency via setter
    public function setLogger(LoggerInterface $logger)
    {
        $this->logger = $logger;
        return $this;
    }
    
    public function update($id)
    {
        // Use logger if available
        if ($this->logger) {
            $this->logger->info('Updating user', ['id' => $id]);
        }
    }
}

// Usage
$controller = new UserController();
$controller->setLogger(new FileLogger());

Interface Injection

php

// Define interface
interface InjectLoggerInterface
{
    public function injectLogger(LoggerInterface $logger);
}

// Implement interface
class UserController extends Controller implements InjectLoggerInterface
{
    private $logger;
    
    public function injectLogger(LoggerInterface $logger)
    {
        $this->logger = $logger;
    }
}

// Container automatically injects
$container->make(UserController::class); // Logger automatically injected

Method Injection

php 

class ReportController extends Controller
{
    // Dependencies injected at method level
    public function generate(ReportGenerator $generator, ExportService $exporter)
    {
        $report = $generator->generate();
        return $exporter->export($report);
    }
}

Type Hinting with Interfaces


// Define interfaces
interface UserRepositoryInterface
{
    public function find($id);
    public function save(User $user);
}

interface MailerInterface
{
    public function send($to, $subject, $body);
}

// Implement interfaces
class DatabaseUserRepository implements UserRepositoryInterface
{
    public function find($id) { /* ... */ }
    public function save(User $user) { /* ... */ }
}

class SmtpMailer implements MailerInterface
{
    public function send($to, $subject, $body) { /* ... */ }
}

// Controller depends on interfaces
class UserController extends Controller
{
    private $userRepository;
    private $mailer;
    
    public function __construct(
        UserRepositoryInterface $userRepository,
        MailerInterface $mailer
    ) {
        $this->userRepository = $userRepository;
        $this->mailer = $mailer;
    }
}

// Bind interfaces to implementations in container
$container->bind(UserRepositoryInterface::class, DatabaseUserRepository::class);
$container->bind(MailerInterface::class, SmtpMailer::class);

Benefits of Dependency Injection

  1. Testability - Easy to mock dependencies during unit testing
  2. Loose Coupling - Classes don't need to know how to create dependencies
  3. Flexibility - Swap implementations without changing dependent classes
  4. Single Responsibility - Classes focus on their core logic, not object creation
  5. Reusability - Dependencies can be shared across multiple classes

Unit Testing with DI


// Test class
class UserControllerTest extends TestCase
{
    public function testShow()
    {
        // Create mock dependencies
        $mockService = $this->createMock(UserService::class);
        $mockService->method('getUserById')
                    ->willReturn(['id' => 1, 'name' => 'John']);
        
        // Inject mock
        $controller = new UserController($mockService);
        
        $result = $controller->show(1);
        
        $this->assertEquals(['id' => 1, 'name' => 'John'], $result);
    }
}

Automatic Dependency Resolution

The container automatically resolves dependencies:

// No need to manually instantiate
$controller = $container->make(UserController::class);

// Container will:
// 1. See UserController needs UserService
// 2. See UserService needs UserRepository and MailService
// 3. Recursively resolve all dependencies
// 4. Inject them automatically

Smarty Engine Templating

JephyPHP Framework Documentation integrates Smarty as its primary template engine, providing clean, secure, and feature-rich templating capabilities.

Why Smarty?

  • Logic-free templates - No PHP in your views
  • Automatic escaping - Built-in XSS protection
  • Template caching - Improved performance
  • Layout inheritance - DRY templates
  • Custom functions - Extensible functionality

Basic Template Structure


php

{* jephy-mvc/app/views/layouts/app.tpl *}
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <title>{$pageTitle|default:"Jephy-MVC"}</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="{asset('css/style.css')}">
</head>
<body>
    <header>
        {include file="partials/header.tpl"}
    </header>
    
    <main>
        {block name="content"}{/block}
    </main>
    
    <footer>
        {include file="partials/footer.tpl"}
    </footer>
</body>
</html>

{* jephy-mvc/app/views/users/index.tpl *}
{extends file="layouts/app.tpl"}

{block name="content"}
    <h1>{$pageTitle} 0}
        <ul>
        {foreach $users as $user}
            <li>
                <a href="/users/{$user->id}">{$user->name|escape}</a>
                - {$user->email|escape}
            </li>
        {/foreach}
        </ul>
    {else}
        <p>No users found. </p>
    {/if}
    
    {include file="partials/pagination.tpl" pagination=$pagination}
{/block}

Variables and Data Passing


php

// In controller
class UserController extends Controller
{
    public function index()
    {
        $users = User::all();
        
        // Assign single variable
        $this->view->assign('pageTitle', 'User List');
        
        // Assign array of variables
        $this->view->assign([
            'users' => $users,
            'total' => count($users),
            'isAdmin' => true
        ]);
        
        // Render template
        return $this->view->display('users/index.tpl');
    }
}


Smarty

{* Accessing variables in template *}
{$pageTitle}
{$users}
{$user->name}
{$array.key}
{$object->property}

{* Default values *}
{$title|default:"No Title"}

{* Escape output (XSS protection) *}
{$userInput|escape}
{$userInput|escape:'html'}
{$userInput|escape:'url'}

{* Variable modifiers *}
{$name|upper}           {* UPPERCASE *}
{$name|lower}           {* lowercase *}
{$date|date_format:"%Y-%m-%d"}
{$number|string_format:"%02d"}
{$text|truncate:50:"..."}
{$html|strip_tags}

Control Structures


Smarty

{* Conditional statements *}
{if $user.isAdmin}
    <a href="/admin">Admin Panel</a>
{elseif $user.isEditor}
    <a href="/editor">Editor Panel</a>
{else}
    <a href="/profile">Profile</a>
{/if}

{* Loops *}
{foreach $users as $user}
<tr>
	<td>{$user@iteration}</td>   {* Current iteration (1-based) *}
	<td>{$user@index}</td>       {* Current index (0-based) *}
	<td>{$user@first}</td>       {* Is first iteration *}
	<td>{$user@last}</td>        {* Is last iteration *}
	<td>{$user->name}</td>
</tr>
{forelse}
<tr>
	<td colspan="3">No users found</td>
&glt;/tr>
{/foreach}

{* While loop *}
{while $counter < 10}
    {$counter++}
{/while}

Layouts and Template Inheritance

php

{* Base layout: layouts/base.tpl *}

    <div id="sidebar">
        {block name="sidebar"}
            <ul>
                <li><a href="/">Home</a></li>
            </ul>
        {/block}
    </div>
    
    <div id="content">
        {block name="content"}{/block}
    </div>
    
    {block name="scripts"}{/block}
</body>
</html>

{* Child template *}
{extends file="layouts/base.tpl"}

{block name="title"}User Profile - {$user.name}{/block}

{block name="styles"}
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="/css/profile.css">
{/block}

{block name="sidebar"}
    {* Optionally show parent content *}
    {$smarty.block.parent}
    
    <div class="user-info">
        Welcome, {$user.name}!
    </div>
{/block}

{block name="content"}
    <h1>Profile of {$user.name}</h1>
    <div class="profile-content">
        {* Content here *}
    </div>
{/block}

Partials and Includes


Smarty

{* Include another template *}
{include file="partials/header.tpl"}

{* Pass variables to included template *}
{include file="partials/card.tpl" title="My Card" content="Card content"}

{* Dynamic include *}
{include file="partials/{$templateName}.tpl"}

{* Include with scope control *}
{include file="sidebar.tpl" scope="parent"}    {* Access parent variables *}
{include file="sidebar.tpl" scope="global"}    {* Access global variables *}

Custom Functions and Plugins



// Register custom Smarty function
$smarty->registerPlugin('function', 'csrf_field', function($params, $smarty) {
    $token = $_SESSION['csrf_token'] ?? '';
    return '';
});

// Register block plugin
$smarty->registerPlugin('block', 'cache', function($params, $content, $smarty, &$repeat) {
    if (!$repeat && $content !== null) {
        $key = $params['key'] ?? 'default';
        // Cache logic here
        return $content;
    }
});




{* Using custom function *}
{csrf_field}

{* Function with parameters *}
{button text="Click Me" class="btn-primary"}

{* Block plugin *}
{cache key="user_list" ttl="3600"}
    {foreach $users as $user}
        {$user->name}
    {/foreach}
{/cache}

Template Caching


php

// Enable caching globally
$smarty->caching = true;
$smarty->cache_lifetime = 3600; // 1 hour

// In controller
$this->view->caching = true;
$this->view->cache_lifetime = 86400; // 24 hours
$this->view->display('products/list.tpl');

// Cache by ID
$this->view->display('products/show.tpl', $productId);

// Clear cache
$smarty->clearCache('products/list.tpl');
$smarty->clearAllCache();

Security Features


php

{* Auto-escaping (enabled by default) *}
{$userInput}  {* Automatically escaped *}

{* Manual escaping *}
{$userInput|escape}
{$userInput|escape:'html'}
{$userInput|escape:'javascript'}

{* Disable escaping (use with caution) *}
{$trustedHtml nofilter}

{* Remove HTML tags *}
{$userInput|strip_tags}

{* Safe HTML filtering *}
{$userInput|purify}

Queues

The Queue system allows you to defer time-consuming tasks (like sending emails, processing images, or API calls) to be processed in the background, improving application response time.

Queue Configuration


.conf
// jephy-mvc/config.conf
[queue]
driver = "database"  // database, redis, beanstalkd, sync
connection = "default"
default_queue = "default"

[redis]
host = "127.0.0.1"
port = 6379
database = 1

[beanstalkd]
host = "127.0.0.1"
port = 11300

Creating Jobs


php

// jephy-mvc/app/jobs/SendWelcomeEmail.php
namespace App\Jobs;

use Core\Queue\Job;
use App\Services\MailService;

class SendWelcomeEmail extends Job
{
    protected $user;
    protected $email;
    
    public function __construct($user)
    {
        $this->user = $user;
        $this->email = $user->email;
    }
    
    public function handle(MailService $mailer)
    {
        // This runs in background
        $mailer->sendWelcomeEmail($this->user);
        
        // Log the action
        error_log("Welcome email sent to {$this->email}");
    }
    
    // Optional: Handle failures
    public function failed(\Exception $e)
    {
        error_log("Failed to send welcome email to {$this->email}: " . $e->getMessage());
    }
}

// jephy-mvc/app/jobs/ProcessImage.php
namespace App\Jobs;

use Core\Queue\Job;

class ProcessImage extends Job
{
    protected $imagePath;
    protected $options;
    
    public function __construct($imagePath, $options = [])
    {
        $this->imagePath = $imagePath;
        $this->options = $options;
    }
    
    public function handle()
    {
        // Resize image
        $image = imagecreatefromjpeg($this->imagePath);
        $resized = imagescale($image, 800, 600);
        imagejpeg($resized, $this->imagePath);
        imagedestroy($image);
        imagedestroy($resized);
        
        return true;
    }
}

Dispatching Jobs

php

// In controller or anywhere
use App\Jobs\SendWelcomeEmail;
use App\Jobs\ProcessImage;

class UserController extends Controller
{
    public function store()
    {
        // Create user
        $user = User::create($this->request->post());
        
        // Dispatch job to queue (non-blocking)
        dispatch(new SendWelcomeEmail($user));
        
        // Dispatch with delay (10 minutes)
        dispatch(new SendWelcomeEmail($user))->delay(600);
        
        // Dispatch on specific queue
        dispatch(new ProcessImage($path))->onQueue('images');
        
        // Response sent immediately, email sent in background
        return $this->json(['message' => 'User created', 'user' => $user]);
    }
}

Queue Workers


php

// Run queue worker (CLI command)
php jephy-mvc/cli.php queue:work

// Run with options
php jephy-mvc/cli.php queue:work --queue=high,default --sleep=3 --tries=3

// Run a single job
php jephy-mvc/cli.php queue:work --once

// Process specific queue
php jephy-mvc/cli.php queue:work --queue=emails

Queue Worker Implementation


php

// jephy-mvc/core/Queue/Worker.php
namespace Core\Queue;

class Worker
{
    public function process($queueName = null, $options = [])
    {
        $queue = QueueManager::connection();
        
        while (true) {
            $job = $queue->pop($queueName);
            
            if ($job) {
                try {
                    $this->processJob($job);
                } catch (\Exception $e) {
                    $this->handleFailedJob($job, $e);
                }
            } else {
                // No jobs, sleep for specified seconds
                sleep($options['sleep'] ?? 3);
            }
        }
    }
    
    protected function processJob($job)
    {
        // Set memory limit
        set_time_limit(0);
        
        // Execute job
        $job->handle();
        
        // Delete job from queue
        $job->delete();
        
        echo "Job processed successfully\n";
    }
}

Database Queue Driver

SQL


// Database queue table migration
CREATE TABLE jobs (
    id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    queue VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    payload TEXT NOT NULL,
    attempts INT DEFAULT 0,
    available_at INT NOT NULL,
    created_at INT NOT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE failed_jobs (
    id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    connection VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    queue VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    payload TEXT NOT NULL,
    exception TEXT NOT NULL,
    failed_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

Queue Events and Monitoring


php

// Listen to queue events
class QueueServiceProvider extends ServiceProvider
{
    public function boot()
    {
        // Before job processed
        $this->hookManager->registerHook('queue.processing', function($job) {
            error_log("Processing job: " . get_class($job));
        });
        
        // After job processed
        $this->hookManager->registerHook('queue.processed', function($job) {
            error_log("Completed job: " . get_class($job));
        });
        
        // Job failed
        $this->hookManager->registerHook('queue.failed', function($job, $exception) {
            error_log("Job failed: " . $exception->getMessage());
        });
    }
}

Events

The Events system allows you to subscribe and listen for various events that occur during your application's lifecycle, enabling loose coupling between different parts of your application.

Event System Overview


php

// jephy-mvc/core/EventDispatcher.php
namespace Core;

class EventDispatcher
{
    private $listeners = [];
    
    public function listen($event, $listener, $priority = 0)
    {
        if (!isset($this->listeners[$event])) {
            $this->listeners[$event] = [];
        }
        
        $this->listeners[$event][] = [
            'listener' => $listener,
            'priority' => $priority
        ];
        
        // Sort by priority
        usort($this->listeners[$event], function($a, $b) {
            return $b['priority'] - $a['priority'];
        });
    }
    
    public function dispatch($event, $payload = [])
    {
        if (!isset($this->listeners[$event])) {
            return $payload;
        }
        
        foreach ($this->listeners[$event] as $listener) {
            $result = call_user_func($listener['listener'], $payload);
            if ($result !== null) {
                $payload = $result;
            }
        }
        
        return $payload;
    }
}

Defining Events

php

// jephy-mvc/app/events/UserRegistered.php
namespace App\Events;

use App\Models\User;

class UserRegistered
{
    public $user;
    public $requestData;
    
    public function __construct(User $user, $requestData = [])
    {
        $this->user = $user;
        $this->requestData = $requestData;
    }
}

// jephy-mvc/app/events/OrderShipped.php
namespace App\Events;

use App\Models\Order;

class OrderShipped
{
    public $order;
    public $trackingNumber;
    
    public function __construct(Order $order, $trackingNumber)
    {
        $this->order = $order;
        $this->trackingNumber = $trackingNumber;
    }
}

Creating Listeners

php

// jephy-mvc/app/listeners/SendWelcomeEmail.php
namespace App\Listeners;

use App\Events\UserRegistered;
use App\Services\MailService;

class SendWelcomeEmail
{
    private $mailer;
    
    public function __construct(MailService $mailer)
    {
        $this->mailer = $mailer;
    }
    
    public function handle(UserRegistered $event)
    {
        $this->mailer->sendWelcomeEmail($event->user);
        error_log("Welcome email sent to {$event->user->email}");
    }
}

// jephy-mvc/app/listeners/AssignDefaultRole.php
namespace App\Listeners;

use App\Events\UserRegistered;

class AssignDefaultRole
{
    public function handle(UserRegistered $event)
    {
        $event->user->role = 'user';
        $event->user->save();
        
        return $event;
    }
}

// jephy-mvc/app/listeners/NotifyAdmin.php
namespace App\Listeners;

use App\Events\UserRegistered;
use App\Services\MailService;

class NotifyAdmin
{
    private $mailer;
    
    public function __construct(MailService $mailer)
    {
        $this->mailer = $mailer;
    }
    
    public function handle(UserRegistered $event)
    {
        $this->mailer->sendNotification(
            'admin@example.com',
            'New User Registered',
            "User {$event->user->name} has registered."
        );
    }
}

Event Service Provider


php

// jephy-mvc/app/providers/EventServiceProvider.php
namespace App\Providers;

use Core\ServiceProvider;
use Core\EventDispatcher;

class EventServiceProvider extends ServiceProvider
{
    protected $listen = [
        'App\Events\UserRegistered' => [
            'App\Listeners\SendWelcomeEmail',
            'App\Listeners\AssignDefaultRole',
            'App\Listeners\NotifyAdmin',
        ],
        'App\Events\OrderShipped' => [
            'App\Listeners\SendShipmentEmail',
            'App\Listeners\UpdateInventory',
        ],
    ];
    
    protected $subscribe = [
        'App\Listeners\UserEventSubscriber',
    ];
    
    public function boot()
    {
        $dispatcher = $this->container->make(EventDispatcher::class);
        
        // Register all events
        foreach ($this->listen as $event => $listeners) {
            foreach ($listeners as $listener) {
                $dispatcher->listen($event, function($payload) use ($listener) {
                    $listenerInstance = $this->container->make($listener);
                    
                    if ($payload instanceof $event) {
                        return $listenerInstance->handle($payload);
                    }
                    
                    // Handle payload as array
                    return $listenerInstance->handle($payload);
                });
            }
        }
    }
}

Dispatching Events


php

// In controller or model
use App\Events\UserRegistered;
use Core\EventDispatcher;

class UserController extends Controller
{
    private $dispatcher;
    
    public function __construct(EventDispatcher $dispatcher)
    {
        $this->dispatcher = $dispatcher;
    }
    
    public function store()
    {
        // Create user
        $user = User::create($this->request->post());
        
        // Create event
        $event = new UserRegistered($user, $this->request->post());
        
        // Dispatch event (all listeners will run)
        $this->dispatcher->dispatch($event);
        
        return $this->json(['message' => 'User created']);
    }
}

// Helper function
function event($event, $payload = [])
{
    $dispatcher = Container::getInstance()->make(EventDispatcher::class);
    
    if (is_string($event)) {
        $event = new $event($payload);
    }
    
    return $dispatcher->dispatch($event);
}

// Usage
event(new UserRegistered($user));

Event Subscribers


php

// jephy-mvc/app/listeners/UserEventSubscriber.php
namespace App\Listeners;

use App\Events\UserRegistered;
use App\Events\UserDeleted;
use App\Events\UserUpdated;

class UserEventSubscriber
{
    public function onUserRegistered($event)
    {
        error_log("User registered: {$event->user->email}");
    }
    
    public function onUserUpdated($event)
    {
        error_log("User updated: {$event->user->id}");
    }
    
    public function onUserDeleted($event)
    {
        error_log("User deleted: {$event->userId}");
    }
    
    public function subscribe($events)
    {
        $events->listen(UserRegistered::class, [$this, 'onUserRegistered']);
        $events->listen(UserUpdated::class, [$this, 'onUserUpdated']);
        $events->listen(UserDeleted::class, [$this, 'onUserDeleted']);
    }
}

Model Events


php

// In your Model class
class User extends Model
{
    // Model event callbacks
    public static function boot()
    {
        parent::boot();
        
        static::creating(function($user) {
            // Before user is created
            $user->api_token = bin2hex(random_bytes(32));
        });
        
        static::created(function($user) {
            // After user is created
            event(new UserRegistered($user));
        });
        
        static::updating(function($user) {
            // Before user is updated
            error_log("User {$user->id} is being updated");
        });
        
        static::deleting(function($user) {
            // Before user is deleted
            if ($user->is_admin) {
                return false; // Prevent deletion
            }
        });
    }
}

Built-in Framework Events


php

// Framework dispatches these events automatically
// jephy-mvc/core/Framework.php

// Before request handling
$this->dispatcher->dispatch('request.starting', $request);

// After route matched
$this->dispatcher->dispatch('route.matched', $route);

// Before controller execution
$this->dispatcher->dispatch('controller.before', $controller);

// After controller execution
$this->dispatcher->dispatch('controller.after', $response);

// Before response sent
$this->dispatcher->dispatch('response.sending', $response);

// After response sent
$this->dispatcher->dispatch('response.sent');

// On exception
$this->dispatcher->dispatch('exception.thrown', $exception);

Listening to Framework Events


php

// jephy-mvc/app/listeners/FrameworkEventSubscriber.php
namespace App\Listeners;

class FrameworkEventSubscriber
{
    public function onRequestStarting($request)
    {
        // Log request
        error_log("Request started: {$request->method()} {$request->uri()}");
    }
    
    public function onResponseSending($response)
    {
        // Add custom header
        header('X-Powered-By: Jephy-MVC');
    }
    
    public function onException($exception)
    {
        // Log exception to external service
        if ($exception instanceof \PDOException) {
            error_log("Database error: " . $exception->getMessage());
        }
    }
    
    public function subscribe($events)
    {
        $events->listen('request.starting', [$this, 'onRequestStarting']);
        $events->listen('response.sending', [$this, 'onResponseSending']);
        $events->listen('exception.thrown', [$this, 'onException']);
    }
}

Service Container

The Service Container (already covered in Architecture section) is a powerful tool for managing class dependencies and performing dependency injection. See the Architecture section for detailed implementation.

Key Container Methods


php

// Register a binding
$container->bind('UserService', function($container) {
    return new UserService($container->make('UserRepository'));
});

// Register a singleton
$container->singleton('Config', function($container) {
    return Config::getInstance();
});

// Resolve from container
$userService = $container->make('UserService');

// Check if bound
if ($container->has('UserService')) {
    // ...
}

// Get all registered bindings
$bindings = $container->getBindings();

View/Template Engine

JephyPHP Framework Documentation uses Smarty as its primary template engine, but the View layer is abstracted to support multiple template engines.

View Abstraction


php

// jephy-mvc/core/View.php
namespace Core;

interface ViewEngine
{
    public function assign($key, $value);
    public function render($template, $data = []);
    public function display($template);
}

class SmartyEngine implements ViewEngine
{
    private $smarty;
    
    public function __construct()
    {
        $this->smarty = new \Smarty();
        $this->smarty->setTemplateDir(Config::get('smarty.template_dir'));
        $this->smarty->setCompileDir(Config::get('smarty.compile_dir'));
        $this->smarty->setCacheDir(Config::get('smarty.cache_dir'));
    }
    
    public function assign($key, $value)
    {
        $this->smarty->assign($key, $value);
        return $this;
    }
    
    public function render($template, $data = [])
    {
        foreach ($data as $key => $value) {
            $this->assign($key, $value);
        }
        
        return $this->smarty->fetch($template);
    }
    
    public function display($template)
    {
        $this->smarty->display($template);
    }
}

class PlainPhpEngine implements ViewEngine
{
    private $data = [];
    
    public function assign($key, $value)
    {
        $this->data[$key] = $value;
        return $this;
    }
    
    public function render($template, $data = [])
    {
        $data = array_merge($this->data, $data);
        extract($data);
        
        ob_start();
        include $template;
        return ob_get_clean();
    }
    
    public function display($template)
    {
        echo $this->render($template);
    }
}

Using Different Engines


php


// config.conf
[view]
engine = "smarty"  // smarty, plainphp, twig

// In controller
class UserController extends Controller
{
    public function index()
    {
        $users = User::all();
        
        // Works with any engine
        $this->view->assign('users', $users);
        $this->view->display('users/index');
        
        // OR
        return $this->view->render('users/index', ['users' => $users]);
    }
}

View Composers


php

// Share data across multiple views
class ViewComposer
{
    public function compose($view)
    {
        $view->assign('appName', Config::get('app.name'));
        $view->assign('currentUser', Auth::user());
        $view->assign('notifications', Notification::unread());
    }
}

// Register composer
View::composer('*', 'App\Composers\GlobalComposer');
View::composer(['users.*', 'profile.*'], 'App\Composers\UserComposer');

Models

Models represent your data structure and business logic. They extend the base Model class which provides Active Record functionality.

Basic Model


php

// jephy-mvc/app/models/Product.php
namespace App\Models;

use Core\Model;

class Product extends Model
{
    protected $table = 'products';
    protected $primaryKey = 'id';
    protected $fillable = ['name', 'price', 'description', 'category_id'];
    protected $hidden = [];
    protected $guarded = ['id', 'created_at', 'updated_at'];
    
    // Cast attributes to specific types
    protected $casts = [
        'price' => 'float',
        'is_active' => 'boolean',
        'created_at' => 'datetime',
        'metadata' => 'array'
    ];
    
    // Accessors
    public function getNameAttribute($value)
    {
        return ucfirst($value);
    }
    
    // Mutators
    public function setPriceAttribute($value)
    {
        $this->attributes['price'] = round($value, 2);
    }
    
    // Virtual attributes
    public function getFormattedPriceAttribute()
    {
        return '$' . number_format($this->price, 2);
    }
}

Model Relationships


php

class Product extends Model
{
    // Belongs To
    public function category()
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(Category::class, 'category_id');
    }
    
    // Has Many
    public function reviews()
    {
        return $this->hasMany(Review::class, 'product_id');
    }
    
    // Belongs To Many
    public function tags()
    {
        return $this->belongsToMany(Tag::class, 'product_tags', 'product_id', 'tag_id');
    }
    
    // Has One Through
    public function inventory()
    {
        return $this->hasOneThrough(Inventory::class, Warehouse::class);
    }
    
    // Polymorphic
    public function images()
    {
        return $this->morphMany(Image::class, 'imageable');
    }
}

// Usage
$product = Product::with('category', 'reviews')->find(1);
echo $product->category->name;
foreach ($product->reviews as $review) {
    echo $review->comment;
}

Classes

Classes in JephyPHP Framework Documentation follow PSR-4 autoloading and are organized by their responsibilities.

Core Classes Structure


jephy-mvc/
├── core/
│   ├── Framework.php      # Main application class
│   ├── Controller.php     # Base controller
│   ├── Model.php          # Base model
│   ├── Router.php         # Routing engine
│   ├── Request.php        # HTTP request handler
│   ├── Response.php       # HTTP response handler
│   ├── Container.php      # Service container
│   ├── Config.php         # Configuration manager
│   ├── Database.php       # Database connection
│   ├── Session.php        # Session management
│   ├── Auth.php           # Authentication
│   ├── Validation.php     # Validation rules
│   ├── Middleware.php     # Base middleware
│   ├── EventDispatcher.php # Event system
│   ├── Queue.php          # Queue system
│   └── ...               # And others

Creating Your Own Classes


// jephy-mvc/app/classes/PaymentProcessor.php
namespace App\Classes;

class PaymentProcessor
{
    private $gateway;
    private $config;
    
    public function __construct($gateway, array $config)
    {
        $this->gateway = $gateway;
        $this->config = $config;
    }
    
    public function process($amount, $currency = 'USD')
    {
        // Payment logic
        return [
            'success' => true,
            'transaction_id' => uniqid('txn_'),
            'amount' => $amount
        ];
    }
}

// Using the class
$processor = new PaymentProcessor('stripe', ['api_key' => 'sk_test_...']);
$result = $processor->process(99.99, 'USD');

Class Autoloading


{
    "name": "jephy/mvc",
    "require": {
        "smarty/smarty": "^4.0",
        "phpmailer/phpmailer": "^6.8",
        "nesbot/carbon": "^3.11",
        "firebase/php-jwt": "^7.0"
    },
    "autoload": {
        "psr-4": {
            "App\\Core\\": "jephy-mvc/core",
            "App\\Core\\Services\\": "jephy-mvc/core/services",
            "App\\Controllers\\": "jephy-mvc/app/controllers",
            "App\\Entities\\": "jephy-mvc/app/entities",
            "App\\Classes\\": "jephy-mvc/app/classes",
            "App\\Hooks\\": "jephy-mvc/app/hooks",
            "App\\Traits\\": "jephy-mvc/app/traits",
            "App\\Image\\": "jephy-mvc/app/image",
            "App\\Models\\": "jephy-mvc/app/models",
            "App\\Listeners\\": "jephy-mvc/app/listeners",
            "App\\Providers\\": "jephy-mvc/app/providers",
            "App\\Repositories\\": "jephy-mvc/app/repositories",
            "App\\Services\\": "jephy-mvc/app/services",
            "App\\Middleware\\": "jephy-mvc/app/middleware",
            "App\\GraphQL\\Enums\\": "jephy-mvc/app/graphql/enums",
            "App\\GraphQL\\Inputs\\": "jephy-mvc/app/graphql/inputs",
            "App\\GraphQL\\Mutations\\": "jephy-mvc/app/graphql/mutations",
            "App\\GraphQL\\Objects\\": "jephy-mvc/app/graphql/objects",
            "App\\GraphQL\\Queries\\": "jephy-mvc/app/graphql/queries",
            "App\\Core\\GraphQL\\Config\\": "jephy-mvc/core/graphql/config",
            "App\\Core\\GraphQL\\Shield\\": "jephy-mvc/core/graphql/shield",
            "App\\Core\\GraphQL\\Shield\\Middleware\\": "jephy-mvc/core/graphql/shield/middleware"
        },
        "files": [
            "jephy-mvc/core/Helper.php"
        ]
    }
}

Entities

Entities represent domain objects with identity and business logic. Unlike models, entities are database-agnostic and focus purely on business rules.

Entity vs Model

  • Model: Database-centric, Active Record pattern
  • Entity: Domain-centric, focuses on business rules, no database logic

Basic Entity Example


// jephy-mvc/app/entities/UserEntity.php
namespace App\Entities;

class UserEntity
{
    private $id;
    private $name;
    private $email;
    private $password;
    private $role;
    private $createdAt;
    
    public function __construct($name, $email, $role = 'user')
    {
        $this->setName($name);
        $this->setEmail($email);
        $this->setRole($role);
        $this->createdAt = new \DateTime();
    }
    
    // Getters
    public function getId() { return $this->id; }
    public function getName() { return $this->name; }
    public function getEmail() { return $this->email; }
    public function getRole() { return $this->role; }
    
    // Setters with validation
    public function setName($name)
    {
        if (strlen($name) < 2) {
            throw new \InvalidArgumentException('Name must be at least 2 characters');
        }
        $this->name = $name;
        return $this;
    }
    
    public function setEmail($email)
    {
        if (!filter_var($email, FILTER_VALIDATE_EMAIL)) {
            throw new \InvalidArgumentException('Invalid email address');
        }
        $this->email = $email;
        return $this;
    }
    
    public function setRole($role)
    {
        $allowedRoles = ['user', 'editor', 'admin'];
        if (!in_array($role, $allowedRoles)) {
            throw new \InvalidArgumentException("Invalid role: {$role}");
        }
        $this->role = $role;
        return $this;
    }
    
    // Business logic methods
    public function changePassword($newPassword)
    {
        if (strlen($newPassword) < 6) {
            throw new \InvalidArgumentException('Password must be at least 6 characters');
        }
        $this->password = password_hash($newPassword, PASSWORD_DEFAULT);
        return $this;
    }
    
    public function verifyPassword($password)
    {
        return password_verify($password, $this->password);
    }
    
    public function isAdmin()
    {
        return $this->role === 'admin';
    }
    
    public function promoteToAdmin()
    {
        $this->setRole('admin');
        return $this;
    }
    
    public function toArray()
    {
        return [
            'id' => $this->id,
            'name' => $this->name,
            'email' => $this->email,
            'role' => $this->role,
            'created_at' => $this->createdAt->format('Y-m-d H:i:s')
        ];
    }
}

Entity Repository


php

// jephy-mvc/app/repositories/UserEntityRepository.php
namespace App\Repositories;

use App\Entities\UserEntity;

class UserEntityRepository
{
    private $db;
    
    public function find($id)
    {
        $data = $this->db->find('users', $id);
        
        if (!$data) {
            return null;
        }
        
        $user = new UserEntity($data['name'], $data['email'], $data['role']);
        
        // Hydrate entity
        $reflection = new \ReflectionClass($user);
        $prop = $reflection->getProperty('id');
        $prop->setAccessible(true);
        $prop->setValue($user, $data['id']);
        
        return $user;
    }
    
    public function save(UserEntity $user)
    {
        if ($user->getId()) {
            // Update
            return $this->db->update('users', $user->getId(), $user->toArray());
        } else {
            // Insert
            $id = $this->db->insert('users', $user->toArray());
            $reflection = new \ReflectionClass($user);
            $prop = $reflection->getProperty('id');
            $prop->setAccessible(true);
            $prop->setValue($user, $id);
            return $id;
        }
    }
}

Value Objects


php

// jephy-mvc/app/entities/valueobjects/Email.php
namespace App\Entities\ValueObjects;

class Email
{
    private $value;
    
    public function __construct($email)
    {
        if (!filter_var($email, FILTER_VALIDATE_EMAIL)) {
            throw new \InvalidArgumentException("Invalid email: {$email}");
        }
        $this->value = $email;
    }
    
    public function getValue()
    {
        return $this->value;
    }
    
    public function getDomain()
    {
        return substr(strrchr($this->value, "@"), 1);
    }
    
    public function equals(Email $other)
    {
        return $this->value === $other->getValue();
    }
    
    public function __toString()
    {
        return $this->value;
    }
}

// jephy-mvc/app/entities/valueobjects/Money.php
class Money
{
    private $amount;
    private $currency;
    
    public function __construct($amount, $currency = 'USD')
    {
        if (!is_numeric($amount)) {
            throw new \InvalidArgumentException('Amount must be numeric');
        }
        $this->amount = (float) $amount;
        $this->currency = strtoupper($currency);
    }
    
    public function getAmount() { return $this->amount; }
    public function getCurrency() { return $this->currency; }
    
    public function add(Money $other)
    {
        if ($this->currency !== $other->getCurrency()) {
            throw new \InvalidArgumentException('Cannot add different currencies');
        }
        return new Money($this->amount + $other->getAmount(), $this->currency);
    }
    
    public function subtract(Money $other)
    {
        if ($this->currency !== $other->getCurrency()) {
            throw new \InvalidArgumentException('Cannot subtract different currencies');
        }
        return new Money($this->amount - $other->getAmount(), $this->currency);
    }
    
    public function format()
    {
        return $this->currency . ' ' . number_format($this->amount, 2);
    }
}

// Using value objects in entity
class ProductEntity
{
    private $name;
    private $price; // Money object
    private $sku;
    
    public function setPrice(Money $price)
    {
        $this->price = $price;
        return $this;
    }
    
    public function applyDiscount($percentage)
    {
        $discount = $this->price->getAmount() * ($percentage / 100);
        $newAmount = $this->price->getAmount() - $discount;
        $this->price = new Money($newAmount, $this->price->getCurrency());
        return $this;
    }
}

Aggregates


php

// jephy-mvc/app/entities/OrderAggregate.php
namespace App\Entities;

use App\Entities\ValueObjects\Money;

class OrderAggregate
{
    private $id;
    private $customer;
    private $items = [];
    private $status = 'pending';
    private $total;
    
    public function __construct(UserEntity $customer)
    {
        $this->customer = $customer;
        $this->total = new Money(0);
    }
    
    public function addItem(ProductEntity $product, $quantity = 1)
    {
        $this->items[] = [
            'product' => $product,
            'quantity' => $quantity,
            'price' => $product->getPrice()
        ];
        
        $this->recalculateTotal();
        return $this;
    }
    
    public function removeItem($productId)
    {
        foreach ($this->items as $index => $item) {
            if ($item['product']->getId() === $productId) {
                unset($this->items[$index]);
                $this->items = array_values($this->items);
                $this->recalculateTotal();
                break;
            }
        }
        return $this;
    }
    
    public function submit()
    {
        if (count($this->items) === 0) {
            throw new \RuntimeException('Cannot submit empty order');
        }
        
        $this->status = 'submitted';
        return $this;
    }
    
    public function pay()
    {
        $this->status = 'paid';
        return $this;
    }
    
    public function ship()
    {
        if ($this->status !== 'paid') {
            throw new \RuntimeException('Cannot ship unpaid order');
        }
        $this->status = 'shipped';
        return $this;
    }
    
    private function recalculateTotal()
    {
        $total = new Money(0);
        foreach ($this->items as $item) {
            $itemTotal = new Money(
                $item['price']->getAmount() * $item['quantity'],
                $item['price']->getCurrency()
            );
            $total = $total->add($itemTotal);
        }
        $this->total = $total;
    }
    
    public function getTotal()
    {
        return $this->total;
    }
    
    public function getStatus()
    {
        return $this->status;
    }
}

Summary

Concept Purpose Key Features
Routing Map URLs to controllers Parameters, groups, middleware, named routes
ORM (Active Record) Database interaction CRUD, relationships, scopes, soft deletes
Dependency Injection Manage class dependencies Constructor injection, container, interfaces
Smarty Engine Template rendering Layouts, caching, security, custom functions
Queues Background job processing Multiple drivers, delays, retries, failure handling
Events Loose coupling, extensibility Listeners, subscribers, model events
Service Container Dependency management Bindings, singletons, automatic resolution
View/Template Engine Presentation layer Multiple engine support, composers
Models Data + database logic Active Record, relationships, casting
Classes Application building blocks PSR-4 autoloading, organized structure
Entities Domain business logic Validation, value objects, aggregates

ON THIS PAGE
